item.title: Wireless-Powered Full-Azimuth Podded Thruster
item.description:
This project develops a wireless-powered full-azimuth podded thruster for unmanned vessels. It targets sealing reliability and maintenance challenges in conventional podded propulsion systems by transferring power wirelessly into the sealed propulsion unit.

Key responsibilities included wireless power transfer system modeling and parameter design, high-frequency wireless power converter control and integration, motor drive control, sealed mechanical structure design, 3D modeling, prototype integration, debugging, and validation.

System highlights include a 1200 W / 100 V GaN motor driver, a 5 kW integrated WPT inverter, a 5-10 cm transfer distance, 92% peak efficiency, and a sealed mechanical structure.

item.title: Active-Rectifier Wireless Motor System
item.description:
This research studies an active-rectifier wireless motor system with dual-side phase shift control. It focuses on coordinated control of the wireless power transfer stage and motor-side energy conversion for compact wireless motor drive applications.

Related paper:

item.title: ELM-Based Fixed-Time Voltage Regulation
item.description:
This research applies an extreme-learning-machine-based adaptive practical fixed-time control method to voltage regulation in wireless power transfer systems. The work targets robust regulation under nonlinear dynamics and parameter uncertainty.

Related paper:
